AutoRenew.description: Whether to automatically renew the subscription instance after expiration.

ChargeEndTime.description: Instance billing end time. The time format is YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:MM:SS'Z'.

ChargeExpireTime.description: Expiration time of the subscription instance. The time format is YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:MM:SS'Z'.

ChargeStartTime.description: Instance billing start time. The time format is YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:MM:SS'Z'.

ChargeStatus.description: Instance billing status. Includes: Normal: normal, Overdue: pay-as-you-go overdue, Expired: subscription expired.

ChargeType.description: Instance billing type. Supported types include: PostPaid: pay-as-you-go, PrePaid: subscription.

OverdueReclaimTime.description: Estimated release time after the instance is suspended due to overdue payment. Time format: YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:MM:SS'Z'.

OverdueTime.description: Instance suspension time due to overdue payment. Time format: YYYY-MM-DD'T'HH:MM:SS'Z'.

Period.description: Subscription instance purchase duration. When PeriodUnit is set to Month, the value range is 1–9. When PeriodUnit is set to Year, the value range is 1–3.

PeriodUnit.description: Unit for purchase duration. Values: MONTHLY: monthly purchase, YEARLY: annual purchase.

EndpointType.description: Type of instance endpoint. RabbitMQ instances provide the following endpoint types: WEB: Web UI endpoint, AMQP0_9_1: AMQP endpoint, MQTT: MQTT endpoint, WEB_MQTT: WEB and MQTT endpoints, STOMP: STOMP endpoint.

InternalEndpoint.description: Private network access domain name of the instance.

NetworkType.description: Network type of the endpoint. PrivateNetwork indicates a private network (VPC).

PublicEndpoint.description: Instance public access domain name. Displayed only after public access is enabled.

description: RabbitMQ Message Queue is a distributed, high-availability message queue product that supports the AMQP (Advanced Message Queuing Protocol) protocol. It is fully compatible with the RabbitMQ open-source ecosystem and multi-language clients, provides rich messaging features and flexible message routing, and offers the advantages of distributed architecture, high throughput, low latency, and flexible scalability as a cloud messaging service. No user deployment or maintenance is required, enabling easy and rapid cloud adoption.

AccountId.description: Main account ID used to create the instance.

ApplyPrivateDNSToPublic.description: Whether public DNS resolution is enabled. true: enabled, false: disabled.

ArchType.description: Instance type, either cluster or standalone.

ChargeDetail.description: Instance billing method and related billing information.

ComputeSpec.description: Compute specification of the RabbitMQ instance.

IsEncrypted.description: Whether cloud disk encryption is enabled.

ProjectName.description: IAM project to which the instance belongs.

StorageSpace.description: Total storage space of the instance, measured in GiB.

UsedStorageSpace.description: Used storage space of the instance, measured in GiB.

Version.description: Supported RabbitMQ versions. Currently supported versions include: 3.8.18: RabbitMQ version 3.8.18.

ZoneId.description: Availability zone ID where the instance is located. For high-availability instances across AZs, multiple availability zone IDs are returned.
